div.p: To all Christ's faithful who shall see or hear the present text, Alexander of Seton, knight, son of Sir Saer of Seton, greeting in the Lord. Let your whole community know [that I] have given, granted, and by this my present charter made firm, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and the monks in that place serving God now and in the future, for ever [for the souls] of all my ancestors and successors, and for my own salvation and my wife's, and my heirs', the whole land that I had in the territory of Ruchlaw near the water of . . . from either side of the same water, by these boundaries: beginning at the Biel ford abover the upper millpond at 'Edmunistoun', and from there going up as far as 'Graycragge', and from 'Graycragge' eastwards, by the foot of the
